title: 10 best herbal essential oils for stomach growling
intro_desc: Herbal essential oils for stomach growling are natural remedies derived from plant extracts that help alleviate stomach discomfort, nausea, and hunger pangs. These oils work by stimulating digestion, reducing inflammation, and promoting relaxation. The best herbal essential oils for stomach growling include Zingiber officinale, Foeniculum vulgare, Cinnamomum verum, Glycyrrhiza glabra, Cuminum cyminum, Curcuma longa, Mentha x piperita, Coriandrum sativum, Cymbopogon citratus, and Anethum graveolens. If left untreated, stomach growling can lead to digestive issues, poor nutrition, and decreased productivity, negatively impacting daily life and overall well-being.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel essential oils helps with stomach growling because of its natural ability to soothe digestive issues. The oil's active compounds, such as fenchone and anise, have been shown to relax the muscles in the digestive tract, reducing bloating and discomfort associated with indigestion and irritable bowel syndrome. Additionally, fennel essential oils' carminative properties help expel trapped gas, providing rapid relief from stomach growling and discomfort. By promoting healthy digestion, fennel essential oils can bring calm to an otherwise uneasy belly.
remedy_properties: Carminative property: Fennel essential oil helps to release gas bubbles in the digestive tract, reducing bloating and discomfort associated with stomach growling, by soothing the digestive muscles and preventing spasms.

Anti-inflammatory property: Fennel oil has anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce inflammation in the gut, which can contribute to stomach growling, by calming down the digestive system and preventing irritation.

Digestive stimulant property: Fennel essential oil stimulates digestion and improves gut motility, which helps to alleviate stomach growling by promoting the proper movement of food through the digestive tract and reducing stagnation.
remedy_parts: Roots: Fennel roots are used to create a calming and soothing effect on the digestive system, reducing stomach growling and discomfort.
Seeds: Fennel seeds contain compounds that help alleviate symptoms of indigestion and bloating, making them an effective ingredient in medicinal essential oils for stomach health.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried fennel seeds from the foeniculum vulgare plant.
Combine dried fennel seeds with carrier oil in a clean glass jar.
Place the mixture in a warm dark place for infusion.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another container.
Distill the infused oil using a steam distillation apparatus.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint essential oils helps with stomach growling because of its natural ability to soothe and calm the digestive tract. The menthol content in peppermint oil relaxes the muscles in the stomach, reducing feelings of hunger and discomfort. Additionally, peppermint's anti-inflammatory properties help to alleviate digestive issues such as indigestion and bloating, which can contribute to stomach growling. When applied topically or inhaled, peppermint essential oils provide a quick and natural solution for calming an upset stomach and alleviating growling sensations.
remedy_properties: Digestive Stimulant: Peppermint essential oil stimulates digestion and relieves symptoms such as bloating, cramps, and discomfort associated with stomach growling by increasing gastric motility and reducing spasms.
Antispasmodic: Peppermint oil's antispasmodic properties help to relax the muscles in the digestive tract, reducing muscle cramps and spasms that can cause stomach growling and discomfort.
Anti-Inflammatory: Peppermint essential oil has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, which can contribute to stomach growling, bloating, and discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in sesquiterpenes, which have been shown to soothe and calm digestive issues.
Rhyzomes: Contain menthol and other compounds that help alleviate nausea and reduce inflammation in the stomach.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh peppermint leaves from your garden or purchase high-quality dried peppermint leaves from a reputable supplier weighing approximately 200g.
Dry the peppermint leaves using a food dehydrator at 35°c for 24 hours or air drying them in a warm well-ventilated area for 5 days to remove excess moisture.
Steam distill the dried peppermint leaves with 1 liter of water in a glass still at atmospheric pressure for 2 hours collecting the resulting essential oil into a clean glass container.
Filter the essential oil through a coffee filter or cheesecloth to remove any impurities and sediment leaving you with a high-quality mentha x piperita essential oil.
Store the mentha x piperita essential oil in a dark glass bottle tightly sealed and placed in the refrigerator at 2°c to 8°c for up to 6 months using a glass dropper lid for easy dispensing.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemongrass essential oils helps with stomach growling because it has natural digestive properties that can soothe and calm the stomach. Additionally, lemongrass essential oil contains anti-inflammatory compounds that can help to reduce inflammation in the gut, promoting a healthy digestion system and alleviating symptoms of indigestion and gas.

Antispasmodic: Lemongrass essential oil has antispasmodic properties that help relax the muscles in the digestive tract, reducing spasms and cramps that can contribute to stomach growling.

Insecticidal: Lemongrass essential oil has insecticidal properties that help eliminate unwanted bacteria, viruses, and parasites in the digestive system, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with stomach growling.

Filter the resulting essential oil through cheesecloth or a coffee filter to remove impurities and sediment.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger essential oils helps with stomach growling because of its natural digestive properties. The oil's active compound, gingerol, stimulates digestion and alleviates symptoms of indigestion and nausea. When inhaled or applied topically, the aroma of ginger essential oil can help to relax the muscles in the stomach, reducing the sensation of hunger pangs and rumbling sounds. Additionally, its warming and calming effects can also soothe digestive discomfort, providing fast relief from an upset stomach.
remedy_properties: Digestive aid: Ginger essential oil helps to stimulate digestive enzymes, reducing stomach cramps and discomfort associated with indigestion and bloating. This is because it contains compounds like gingerol and shogaol, which have natural anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.

Antispasmodic: The antispasmodic properties of ginger essential oil help to relax the muscles in the digestive tract, reducing spasms and cramps that can cause stomach growling and discomfort. This is because it has a calming effect on the smooth muscle layer of the digestive tract.

Carminative: Ginger essential oil acts as a natural carminative, helping to release trapped gas and reduce bloating by stimulating the release of digestive enzymes and improving digestion. This helps to alleviate stomach growling and discomfort caused by swallowed air or undigested food.
remedy_parts: Roots: The root system of ginger contains bioactive compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, making it effective in soothing digestive issues such as stomach growling.
Rhyzomes: Ginger rhyzomes are rich in gingerols and shogaols, which are responsible for the plant's medicinal properties, including its ability to alleviate stomach discomfort and reduce nausea.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality fresh ginger roots weighing approximately 2-3 pounds.
Wash the ginger roots thoroughly with cold running water to remove any dirt or debris.
Peel the ginger roots using a sharp knife and chop them into small pieces measuring about 1-inch long.
Steam the chopped ginger root pieces in a double boiler for 30-45 minutes at medium heat until fragrant.
Dry the steamed ginger mixture in a food dehydrator or oven at low temperature for 6-8 hours.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh carum carvi seeds at dawn, selecting those with no signs of mold or mildew.
Dry the harvested seeds in a warm, well-ventilated area for three to four hours to reduce moisture content.
Crush 100 grams of dried carum carvi seeds into smaller pieces using a mortar and pestle to release their oils.
Steam distill the crushed seeds with 200 milliliters of distilled water for two to three hours, collecting the resulting essential oil.
Filter the collected essential oil through a coffee filter or cheesecloth to remove impurities and sediment, storing it in dark glass bottles.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh melissa officinalis leaves and stems for the highest quality oil content.
Wash the harvested plant material gently in cold running water to remove dirt and debris.

Steam distill the dried plant material using a clevenger apparatus with a 1:20 ratio of plant material to water for 2 hours.
Filter and bottle the resulting essential oil in dark glass containers to preserve its therapeutic properties.

Grind 100g of dried piper nigrum seeds into fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.

Store the high-quality cinnamomum verum essential oil in dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids at room temperature for up to 12 months.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh leaves of artemisia absinthium and dry them in a single layer for 3-4 hours under shade.
Combine 200g dried artemisia absinthium with 500ml carrier oil such as jojoba or sweet almond oil in a glass jar.
Steep the mixture for 4-6 weeks shaking the jar daily to ensure even infusion of essential oils from herbs into the carrier oil.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or coffee filter to separate the solids from the liquid and discard the solids.
Transfer the extracted herbal essential oil to dark glass bottle with tight-fitting lid and store in cool place for up to 6 months.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried origanum vulgare leaves in the morning for optimal oil quality.

Steep the mixture for 14 days, shaking the jar daily, then strain and discard solids.
Transfer the resulting liquid to a still and distill under vacuum at 100°c for 30 minutes.

Filter the extract through a filter paper and separate the essential oil from the solvent by.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cinnamomum verum essential oils helps with stomach growling because of its natural digestive properties. remedy_recipe: Harvest mature cinnamomum verum branches and remove the bark from the stems to get the desired material for oil extraction.

Crush the dried cinnamomum verum bark into small pieces using a food processor to increase surface area for steam distillation.

remedy_constituents: Menthol: Acts as a local anesthetic, numbing the stomach and reducing the sensation of hunger or stomach growling.
Menthone: Exhibits carminative properties, helping to alleviate gas and digestive discomfort that may contribute to stomach growling.
Limonene: Possesses anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which can help soothe stomach cramps and spasms that may lead to growling.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are the most commonly used part for essential oil production due to their high menthol and menthone content, which provides a quick relief from stomach discomfort.
Stems: The stems are used to produce essential oils with a higher concentration of menthol, which helps to ease stomach growling and digestive issues.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes are used to produce essential oils with a balanced composition of menthol and menthone, making them effective in alleviating stomach discomfort and promoting digestion.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1-2 pounds of fresh mentha x piperita leaves in the morning after the dew has evaporated.
Dry the harvested leaves in a single layer at room temperature for 2-3 weeks.
Steam distill the dried leaves in a 100% glass steam distillation apparatus for 2-3 hours.
Collect the resulting essential oil and separate it from the water using a separatory funnel.
Filter the collected essential oil through a 5-micron filter paper to remove any remaining impurities.

remedies.remedy_desc: Coriandrum sativum essential oils helps with stomach growling because of its natural carminative properties, which aid in relieving digestive discomfort. The oil contains compounds such as linalool and geranyl acetate, which have been traditionally used to ease digestive issues and alleviate hunger pangs. By reducing inflammation and promoting relaxation, Coriandrum sativum essential oils can help calm the stomach and reduce the sensation of hunger, providing relief from stomach growling and discomfort. This makes it a popular natural remedy for digestive issues.
remedy_constituents: Linalool: Acts as a gastroprotective agent, reducing inflammation and soothing the stomach lining to alleviate growling sensations.
Limonene: Exhibits carminative properties, helping to release trapped gas and reduce bloating, which can contribute to stomach growling.
Phellandrene: Demonstrates anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic effects, which can help relax the stomach muscles and reduce discomfort associated with growling.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Leaves are the most commonly used part for making essential oils, as they contain the highest concentration of oils that help to calm digestive issues.
Seeds: Coriander seeds are rich in linalool and geraniol, which are compounds that aid in digestion and reduce stomach growling.
Roots: Coriander roots contain a compound called linalool, which is also found in the seeds and helps to soothe digestive issues, including stomach growling.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh coriandrum sativum leaves and flowers in the early morning after dew has evaporated for better oil quality.
Steam distillation process involves mixing 10 kg of fresh plant material with 20 kg of water in a distillation apparatus.
Heat the mixture at 70-80°c for 2-3 hours to release essential oil from plant cells and separate from water.
Separate the essential oil from the distillate by decantation and filter it through a paper filter to remove impurities.
Store the essential oil in dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids to protect it from light and oxidation for up to 12 months.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cymbopogon citratus essential oils helps with stomach growling because it has natural anti-inflammatory and digestive properties. The oil's active compounds, such as citronellal and geraniol, have been shown to soothe the digestive tract and reduce inflammation in the stomach. As a result, it can help to calm stomach growling and discomfort, promoting a sense of relief and digestive balance. This can be especially beneficial for individuals experiencing digestive issues, such as bloating or cramps.
remedy_constituents: Limonene: Helps to alleviate stomach growling by reducing inflammation and improving digestion due to its carminative properties.
Geraniol: Exhibits calming effects on the stomach, reducing muscle spasms and alleviating cramps that contribute to stomach growling.
Borneol: Helps to soothe the digestive system, reducing acidity and relieving stomach discomfort, which can be triggered by growling.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are the most commonly used part, as they contain the highest concentration of citral, a key compound responsible for the oil's digestive benefits.
Stems: The stems of Cymbopogon citratus are also used to produce essential oil, which is said to aid in digestion and alleviate stomach discomfort.
Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes are another part of the plant used to make essential oil, which is believed to have anti-inflammatory properties and help alleviate stomach issues.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh cymbopogon citratus leaves in the early morning after the dew has evaporated.
Dry the harvested leaves in a single layer at 35 degrees celsius for 2 hours.
Steam distill 100 grams of dried leaves in 500 milliliters of distilled water for 3 hours.
Separate the essential oil from the distillate using a separating funnel and store it in a dark glass bottle.
Filter the essential oil through a 5-micron filter paper and then store it in a sealed container.

remedies.remedy_desc: Anethum graveolens essential oils helps with stomach growling because its active compounds, such as pinocamphone and limonene, possess carminative properties that help alleviate digestive discomfort. The oil's ability to ease spasms and relax the muscles in the gastrointestinal tract reduces the frequency and intensity of stomach growling. Additionally, its anti-inflammatory properties may soothe the digestive lining, promoting a healthy gut environment and reducing symptoms associated with stomach growling. This makes Anethum graveolens essential oil a potential natural remedy for digestive issues.
remedy_constituents: Carvone: It helps to reduce stomach growling by acting as a carminative, which means it helps to alleviate gas and bloating in the digestive system.
Dill apiole: It helps to calm the stomach muscles, reducing the spasms and contractions that lead to stomach growling and discomfort.
Limonene: It has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce inflammation in the digestive system and alleviate stomach cramps and growling.
remedy_parts: Seeds: The seeds of Anethum graveolens are used to make essential oils because they contain the highest concentration of dill oil, which has natural carminative and digestive properties.
Leaves: The leaves of Anethum graveolens are used to make essential oils because they have a mild flavor and aroma that helps to ease digestive discomfort and reduce stomach growling.

Stems: The stems of Anethum graveolens are used to make essential oils because they contain a higher concentration of dill's anti-inflammatory compounds, which help to soothe digestive issues and relieve stomach discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest the anethum graveolens seeds when they are fully ripe and the plant is in its flowering stage.
Dry the anethum graveolens seeds in a warm area with good air circulation for 7 to 10 days.
Steam distill the dried anethum graveolens seeds using a distillation apparatus at 100 to 150 degrees celsius for 3 to 4 hours.
Collect the essential oil from the distillation apparatus and separate it from the water using a separatory funnel.
Store the anethum graveolens essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid in a cool place.
